title: General Health Insurance Information/Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > General Health Insurance Information/Counseling 44 Matching Service Providers OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OHP IN-PERSON APPLICATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps people find experts who provide in-person assistance with health insurance enrollment. Health Insurance Agents help with private insurance through the Oregon Health Insurance Marketplace (OHIM), while Community Partners, also called assisters, help with enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP). 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Varies by service (503) 945-5772 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAKER COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERANS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County Veterans Service Offices (CVSOs) are the entry points to understanding the benefits, resources, and entitlements available to veterans, their dependents, and survivors. CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in health care -Assistance for requesting military service documents -Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Oregon Veterans' Home Conservatorship services, including total estate management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law 2200 Fourth Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Veterans with any discharge status other than dishonorable and their immediate family members (spouses, surviving spouses who have not remarried, children, or stepchildren) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm, Friday by appointment Email (541) 523-8223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERANS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County Veterans Service Offices (CVSOs) are the entry points to understanding the benefits, resources, and entitlements available to veterans, their dependents, and survivors. CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in healthcare -Assistance for requesting military service documents -Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Oregon Veterans' Home Conservatorship services, including total estate management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law. 1121 NW 9th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Veterans with any discharge status other than dishonorable and their immediate family members (spouses, surviving spouses who have not remarried, children, or stepchildren) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm, Friday by appointment Email (541) 368-7100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERANS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves as the entry point to understanding throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in health care -Assistance for requesting military service documents -Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans 2051 Kaen Road Suite 281 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Veterans with any discharge status other than dishonorable and their immediate family members (spouse, un-remarried surviving spouse, child, or stepchild) Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 650-5631 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERANS SERVICES Print & Share X Print As navigators through USDVA (VA) systems, CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -VA Education benefit assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in healthcare -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance and information about obtaining VA Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Oregon Veterans' Home and ODVA Conservatorship services (financial management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law) -Speaking engagements to inform and educate about Veterans' benefits 1036 SE Douglas Avenue Room 7/8 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Veterans, their immediate family members (spouse or un-remarried surviving spouse, child, or stepchild). If a Veteran's discharge is Less than Honorable or General Under Honorable Conditions please e-mail or call for paperwork, guidelines, assistance or referral about Discharge Review or upgrade. Also offers help with reporting changes, submitting documents, and resolving complex issues. CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in health care -Assistance for requesting military service documents -Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Oregon Veterans' Home Conservatorship services, including total estate management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law 1407 Monmouth Independence Highway Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Veterans with any discharge status other than dishonorable and their immediate family members (spouse, un-remarried surviving spouse, child, or stepchild) Hours: Tuesday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 751-1644 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERANS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County Veterans Service Offices (CVSOs) serve as the entry points to understanding the benefits, resources, and entitlements available to veterans, their dependents, and survivors.
